quotes.text: My mama always said life was like a box of chocolates. You never know what you're gonna get.
quotes.speaker: Forrest Gump (1994)
quotes.context: Forrest (Tom Hanks) sits on a bus-stop bench recounting his mother's favorite saying.

quotes.speaker: Caddyshack (1980)
quotes.context: Ty Webb (Chevy Chase) giving zen golf advice to caddy Danny Noonan during their night-golf lesson

quotes.text: You keep touching that wound, it's never going to close.
quotes.speaker: Chinatown (1974)
quotes.context: Evelyn Mulwray warning Jake Gittes, speaking both literally and with an edge of weariness
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the double-meaning delivery and clipped fatalism fit Chinatown's noir register perfectly, and Evelyn does tend to Gittes's injuries in the film. The subtle giveaway is that it leans just a hair too metaphorical for her usual guarded, practical speech.

quotes.text: Don't look at me like that. Everybody in this family keeps secrets.
quotes.speaker: The Royal Tenenbaums (2001)
quotes.context: Margot Tenenbaum snapping at Richie after he discovers something she'd hidden
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Margot's flat, defensive delivery and the film's whole architecture of family concealment make this entirely plausible. The subtle giveaway is that she's usually even more withholding than this; outright acknowledging secrets is slightly too self-aware for her.

quotes.text: One does not simply walk into Mordor.
quotes.speaker: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
quotes.context: Boromir (Sean Bean) arguing against the plan to destroy the Ring

quotes.text: Nobody orders the fish on a Monday, counselor. Nobody.
quotes.speaker: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
quotes.context: Hannibal Lecter offering unsolicited, ominous advice to Clarice Starling through the cell glass
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lecter's habit of dispensing precise, condescending little rules in a conversational tone is all over the film, so this lands naturally. The giveaway is that 'counselor' isn't quite his address of choice for Clarice; he prefers her name.
